cs_tickets.subject: Cannot connect to production database after update
cs_tickets.description: After the v2.4.1 update, our production CDC connector is failing with connection timeout errors. The connector was working fine on v2.4.0. Error: `connection refused on port 5432`. Happening for all our PostgreSQL sources.

cs_tickets.subject: Billing charge I don't recognize on April invoice
cs_tickets.description: I see a charge of $299 on my April invoice labeled 'overage fee' but I don't understand what triggered it. My usage dashboard shows I'm within my plan limits. Please clarify and refund if incorrect.

cs_tickets.subject: How do I set up a Snowflake sink with row-level encryption?
cs_tickets.description: We want to configure our Snowflake sink to apply column-level encryption for PII fields before writing. What's the recommended approach using the platform's sink configuration?

cs_tickets.subject: Request: support for MongoDB Atlas serverless in CDC connector
cs_tickets.description: We're migrating our primary database to MongoDB Atlas serverless instances. The current CDC connector only supports dedicated clusters with change streams. Is Atlas serverless on the roadmap?

cs_tickets.subject: Getting started - first CDC pipeline setup
cs_tickets.description: Just signed up and need help getting my first PostgreSQL CDC pipeline running. I've followed the docs but the connector status is showing 'initializing' for the past 20 minutes.

cs_conversations.transcript: Agent: Hi Priya, I can see the issue - v2.4.1 introduced a stricter TLS handshake. Your connector config needs `sslmode=require`. Let me send you the updated config snippet. Customer: Got it, will try now. Agent: The fix is in. Let me know if you need a rollback to v2.4.0 in the meantime. Customer: It's working! Thank you for the quick turnaround.
cs_conversations.resolution_notes: v2.4.1 TLS strictness broke connectors without explicit sslmode. Provided config fix. Added to known issues doc.

cs_conversations.transcript: Agent: Great question on Snowflake encryption. You'll want to use Snowflake's column masking policies at the sink layer rather than in our config. Here's the recommended pattern... Customer: Perfect, that makes sense. Will the sink worker respect the masking policies automatically? Agent: Yes - the sink writes to the table, Snowflake applies the masking policy at query time. Customer: Excellent, exactly what we needed.
cs_conversations.resolution_notes: Directed customer to use Snowflake native column masking. No platform change needed.

cs_conversations.transcript: Agent: Welcome! The 'initializing' status usually means the replication slot is being created. This can take up to 30 minutes for large databases. Is yours a large database? Customer: About 50GB. Agent: That's likely it - give it another 10-15 minutes. You can check progress in the connector logs tab. Customer: It just turned green! Agent: Great, you're all set. Your first events should start flowing within a minute.
cs_conversations.resolution_notes: New customer waiting for replication slot creation. Normal behavior for 50GB database. Added note to onboarding docs to set better expectations.

cs_feedback.feedback_text: Fast response for a critical issue. Would have given 5 stars if I hadn't needed to upgrade configs manually.
Agent knew exactly what I needed and explained it clearly. This is why we chose enterprise support.
Incredibly helpful for a brand new customer. The agent walked me through everything step by step.
Still waiting on resolution for my billing issue. Getting passed between departments.

cs_nps_responses.feedback: The real-time CDC pipelines have replaced our entire batch ETL stack. The support team is responsive and the platform is reliable.
Product is solid but billing transparency is poor. I've had two unexplained charges and support takes forever to respond.
Best data platform we've used. The support team actually reads my ticket before responding.
Great product. Documentation could be more thorough on advanced sink configs.
